cases.title: Herron et al. v. Central Indiana Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC denied most specifications of a complaint filed by TE Herron and others against Central Indiana Presbytery's investigative and suspension procedures, but sustained the complaint on the point that Presbytery improperly denied Herron access to Presbytery minutes while under process. The decision includes extensive discussion of pre-trial BCO 43-1 complaints ('interlocutory appeals') and the proper scope of BCO 31-10 administrative suspensions.

cases.title: Park et al. v. Korean Central Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC sustained a complaint against Korean Central Presbytery for administering a censure to a teaching elder before an indictment was drawn, in violation of BCO 32-3, and declared the underlying matter moot given that events occurred 21 years prior and the victim declined to participate. A concurring opinion argued the case should have been remanded to Presbytery for further action, and a dissenting opinion objected to declaring the case moot.

cases.title: Herron et al. v. Central Indiana Presbytery
cases.synopsis: This complaint was postponed pending resolution of Case 2022-10 (PCA v. Herron trial) per the SJC's June 2022 instruction in Case 2021-06, and the SJC ruled it be decided by reference to the April 5, 2023 Final Decision in Case 2022-10.

cases.title: Barber et al. v. Central Indiana Presbytery
cases.synopsis: This complaint was postponed pending resolution of Case 2022-10 (PCA v. Herron trial) per the SJC's June 2022 instruction in Case 2021-06, and the SJC ruled it be decided by reference to the April 5, 2023 Final Decision in Case 2022-10.

cases.title: Eagle et al. v. Savannah River Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC found the complaint administratively out of order and dismissed it because it was not filed within 30 days of the later of two relevant notification events, rendering it untimely under BCO 43-3. The case was related to an earlier complaint (Case 2020-10) that had also been ruled out of order as prematurely filed.

cases.title: Klett et al. v. Philadelphia Presbytery
precedent_refs: Bogue, et al v. The Presbytery of the Ascension
Gentry, et al v. Calvary Presbytery
Rayburn, et al v. Missouri Presbytery
Landrum, et al v. Mississippi Valley Presbytery
topics: continuing gifts of prophecy and tongues
fundamentals of the system of doctrine
cases.synopsis: The SJC sustained a complaint against Philadelphia Presbytery for failing to find that TE Larry Smith's views affirming continuing gifts of prophecy and tongues (as a 'third category of revelation' beyond special and general revelation) were out of accord with the fundamentals of the system of doctrine because hostile to it, as required by BCO 21-4.f. The case continues past this window's end page.
